report_metadata.cancer_type: Salivary Duct Carcinoma of Parotid Gland

treatments.name: Radical Parotidectomy + Neck Dissection + Facial Nerve Sacrifice with Immediate Reanimation

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN/ASCO guidelines mandate radical surgery for T4a salivary duct carcinoma with facial nerve invasion; multi-institutional analyses of 141+ patients validate approach; standard of care worldwide

survival_benefit.rationale: 3-year OS ~70.5% and DFS ~38.2% for SDC overall; R0 resection is strongest predictor of survival; T4aN2 has poor DFS but surgery remains only curative-intent option; facial nerve sacrifice required for invaded nerve

accessibility.rationale: Available at head and neck surgery centers; requires experienced parotid surgeon for nerve reanimation; immediate facial reanimation preferred at time of sacrifice

safety_profile.rationale: Facial nerve sacrifice causes permanent facial paralysis; immediate reanimation improves functional outcomes; Frey syndrome, wound complications, shoulder dysfunction from neck dissection; nerve grafting from sural or great auricular nerve

biomarker_match.rationale: Surgery indicated regardless of molecular profile for resectable T4a disease; R0 resection enables adjuvant targeted therapy based on AR+ and HER2+ status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Radical parotidectomy with total removal of parotid gland and sacrificed facial nerve segments involved by tumor. Comprehensive neck dissection (levels I-V) addresses N2 nodal disease. Immediate facial reanimation using nerve grafts (sural nerve, great auricular nerve) or sling procedures restores facial symmetry and eye closure.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Radiation Therapy (60-66 Gy) Post-Surgery

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN Category 1 recommendation for high-risk features (T4, N2, perineural invasion, close/positive margins); NCDB analysis shows improved survival with adjuvant RT for salivary gland malignancies

survival_benefit.rationale: Adjuvant RT improves local-regional control in T4aN2 disease; essential given high recurrence risk of SDC; radiation doses 60-66 Gy to tumor bed and involved nodal regions

accessibility.rationale: Radiation therapy widely available; IMRT technique standard for parotid bed; well-established treatment planning protocols for salivary gland malignancies

safety_profile.rationale: Xerostomia (contralateral parotid sparing with IMRT), mucositis, dermatitis, osteoradionecrosis risk, trismus, hearing changes; long-term fibrosis; dental complications requiring pre-RT dental evaluation

biomarker_match.rationale: Indicated for all SDC with T4 or N2 features regardless of molecular profile; perineural invasion from facial nerve invasion further strengthens RT indication

treatments.mechanism_of_action: High-dose external beam radiation therapy (60-66 Gy in 30-33 fractions) using intensity-modulated RT (IMRT) to the parotid bed, involved neck levels, and surgical margins. Targets residual microscopic disease and reduces local-regional recurrence in high-risk SDC. Concurrent cisplatin may be added for positive margins.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / Scientific Reports

treatments.name: Trastuzumab + Docetaxel (HER2-Targeted Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Japanese Phase 2 trial (JFMC44-1101) with 57 patients; ORR 70.2%, CR 14.0%; multicenter Phase 2 confirmation (ORR 60%, OS 33.8 months); strongest evidence for HER2-directed therapy in SDC

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 70.2%, CR 14.0%, CBR 84.2%; median OS 39.7 months; DCR 93.3%; transforms outcomes for HER2 3+ SDC from ~12 months with chemotherapy alone

accessibility.rationale: Trastuzumab and docetaxel both FDA approved; used off-label for SDC based on strong Phase 2 data; widely available at oncology centers; NCCN recommends HER2-directed therapy

safety_profile.rationale: Docetaxel myelosuppression, fatigue, alopecia; trastuzumab cardiotoxicity (LVEF monitoring); peripheral neuropathy; infusion reactions; manageable with standard supportive care

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient is HER2 3+ — optimal target for trastuzumab; SDC with HER2 overexpression (40-83% of cases) shows highest response rates to HER2-directed therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Trastuzumab binds HER2 extracellular domain, blocking receptor dimerization and signaling, while recruiting immune effector cells for ADCC. Docetaxel stabilizes microtubules causing mitotic arrest. Synergistic combination exploits HER2 oncogenic addiction in SDC with HER2 3+ overexpression.

treatments.name: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan (T-DXd / Enhertu) — Tumor-Agnostic HER2-Targeted ADC

evidence_level.rationale: MYTHOS Phase 2 trial in HER2+ salivary gland cancer; DESTINY-PanTumor02 tumor-agnostic data; FDA tumor-agnostic approval August 2024 for HER2+ solid tumors

survival_benefit.rationale: MYTHOS: ORR 61.3% in HER2 IHC 3+; median DOR 22.1 months; median PFS 11.9 months; median OS 21.1 months; superior to conventional chemotherapy

accessibility.rationale: FDA tumor-agnostic approval covers HER2+ SDC after prior therapy; available at oncology centers; IV every 3 weeks; accelerated approval pathway

safety_profile.rationale: Interstitial lung disease (ILD) risk: 10.5% grade 3+ including 3 deaths in MYTHOS; requires close pulmonary monitoring; nausea, fatigue, neutropenia; ILD monitoring mandatory

biomarker_match.rationale: HER2 3+ is the optimal biomarker for T-DXd; tumor-agnostic approval specifically based on IHC 3+; patient's HER2 3+ status directly qualifies for treatment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Trastuzumab deruxtecan is a HER2-directed antibody-drug conjugate with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (deruxtecan/DXd). High drug-to-antibody ratio (8:1) delivers potent cytotoxin directly to HER2+ cells. Bystander effect kills adjacent HER2-low cells through membrane-permeable payload release.

treatments.name: Combined Androgen Blockade (Leuprolide + Bicalutamide)

evidence_level.rationale: Prospective Phase 2 trial (36 patients); nationwide Dutch case series (35 patients); consistent results showing CBR 50-75%; NCCN recommends for AR+ SDC

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 18-41.7%; CBR 50-75% depending on regimen; median OS 17 months with ADT vs 5 months BSC; disease stabilization common; modest but clinically meaningful benefit

accessibility.rationale: GnRH agonists (leuprolide) and antiandrogens (bicalutamide) widely available; oral + injection route; established use from prostate cancer; low cost relative to HER2 therapies

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized ADT side effects: hot flashes, sexual dysfunction, fatigue, bone density loss, metabolic syndrome; generally well-tolerated; long-term safety data from prostate cancer experience

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient is AR+ — direct therapeutic target; SDC has 73-89% AR positivity rate; combined androgen blockade more effective than monotherapy in AR+ SDC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combined androgen blockade (CAB): Leuprolide acetate (GnRH agonist) suppresses testicular testosterone production via pituitary downregulation; bicalutamide (antiandrogen) competitively blocks AR at receptor level. Dual blockade maximizes androgen suppression in AR-dependent SDC tumor growth.
treatments.availability: Off-label (FDA approved components for prostate cancer)

treatments.name: Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab + Docetaxel (Dual HER2 Blockade)

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and small series showing responses; extrapolated from breast cancer paradigm (CLEOPATRA); biologically rational dual HER2 blockade; no Phase 2/3 in SDC

survival_benefit.rationale: Case reports show complete responses lasting 52+ months; dual HER2 blockade may improve depth and duration of response over trastuzumab alone; breast cancer data supports superiority of dual blockade

accessibility.rationale: Both antibodies FDA approved for breast cancer; off-label use in SDC; available at major cancer centers; higher cost with dual antibody therapy

safety_profile.rationale: Dual HER2 blockade increases diarrhea risk; cardiotoxicity monitoring essential with dual anti-HER2; docetaxel myelosuppression; febrile neutropenia risk with triplet

biomarker_match.rationale: HER2 3+ — optimal for dual HER2 blockade; pertuzumab blocks different HER2 domain (domain II dimerization) than trastuzumab (domain IV); complementary mechanisms

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Trastuzumab binds HER2 domain IV blocking ligand-independent signaling; pertuzumab binds HER2 domain II preventing HER2/HER3 heterodimerization. Dual blockade provides more complete HER2 pathway inhibition than either antibody alone. Combined with docetaxel cytotoxic chemotherapy.

key_evidence.journal: Case Reports in Oncology / Clinical Case Reports

treatments.name: Ado-Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1)

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective series and case reports in HER2+ SGC; median treatment duration 8.3 months; used as second-line HER2-directed therapy; limited prospective data in SDC

survival_benefit.rationale: Durable responses reported; median treatment duration 8.3 months (range 1-47 months); provides option after trastuzumab/docetaxel progression; disease control common

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for HER2+ breast cancer; off-label use in SDC; IV every 3 weeks; widely available at oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Better tolerated than docetaxel combinations; thrombocytopenia main concern; hepatotoxicity requires monitoring; no alopecia; lower GI toxicity than T-DXd

biomarker_match.rationale: HER2 3+ — directly targetable with T-DM1; ADC delivers DM1 payload specifically to HER2+ cells; effective after trastuzumab-based treatment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Antibody-drug conjugate combining trastuzumab with DM1 (emtansine/maytansinoid), a microtubule inhibitor. Binds HER2, is internalized, and releases DM1 intracellularly to cause mitotic arrest and cell death. Single-agent activity eliminates need for concurrent chemotherapy.

treatments.name: Cisplatin-Based Chemotherapy (CAP or Cisplatin/Docetaxel)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple Phase 2 trials; 35-46% ORR with cisplatin combinations; longstanding evidence base; NCCN lists as systemic therapy option for recurrent/metastatic SDC

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 35-46% with cisplatin combinations; median response duration 5-7 months; no survival advantage over best supportive care in some studies; inferior to HER2-directed therapy in HER2+ SDC

accessibility.rationale: Cisplatin, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide widely available worldwide; established infusion protocols; low cost relative to targeted therapies

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, myelosuppression, nausea/vomiting, peripheral neuropathy; requires hydration protocol; cumulative dose limitations

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-targeted cytotoxic therapy; no molecular selectivity; inferior to HER2 and AR-directed approaches in biomarker-positive SDC; may serve as backbone for trastuzumab combinations

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Platinum-based chemotherapy causes DNA crosslinking and adduct formation leading to apoptosis. Combined with doxorubicin (DNA intercalation) and cyclophosphamide (alkylation) in CAP regimen, or with docetaxel (microtubule stabilization). Non-targeted cytotoxic approach for advanced SDC.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Anti-PD-1 Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-028 Phase 1b and KEYNOTE-158 Phase 2 in salivary gland cancer; FDA approved for MSI-H/TMB-H tumors; modest activity in unselected SGC (ORR 4.6-12%)

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR only 4.6% overall in KEYNOTE-158; 10.7% in PD-L1+ patients; durable responses in small subset; not effective as single agent for most SDC patients

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications including MSI-H/TMB-H tumors; widely available; IV every 3 weeks; standard oncology infusion

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related AEs; generally well-tolerated; 1 ILD death in KEYNOTE-028; manageable toxicity profile

biomarker_match.rationale: SDC generally has low PD-L1 expression; may benefit from PD-L1+ subset or MSI-H tumors; not specifically matched to AR+ or HER2+ biology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD-1 receptor on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immune response. SDC generally has lower PD-L1 expression than other head and neck cancers, limiting single-agent activity. May synergize with AR blockade by altering tumor microenvironment.

key_evidence.journal: American Journal of Clinical Oncology / European Journal of Cancer

treatments.name: Darolutamide + Goserelin (Next-Generation CAB — DISCOVARY)

evidence_level.rationale: DISCOVARY Phase 2 study; darolutamide + goserelin showed superior results to darolutamide monotherapy; next-generation AR-targeting approach; recent 2025 data

survival_benefit.rationale: Superior response rate and PFS compared to darolutamide monotherapy; supports CAB principle with more potent second-generation antiandrogen; results pending full publication

accessibility.rationale: Darolutamide FDA approved for prostate cancer; goserelin widely available; off-label use in SDC; clinical trial access preferred; higher cost than bicalutamide

safety_profile.rationale: Darolutamide has favorable safety profile in prostate cancer (less fatigue, no seizure risk unlike enzalutamide); standard ADT side effects; well-tolerated combination

biomarker_match.rationale: AR+ status directly targets with next-generation antiandrogen; CAB with darolutamide may overcome resistance to first-generation antiandrogens

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Darolutamide is a structurally distinct second-generation AR antagonist with high affinity for AR and minimal CNS penetration (reduces fatigue/seizure risk). Combined with goserelin (GnRH agonist) for complete androgen blockade. May be more potent than bicalutamide-based CAB in AR+ SDC.

treatments.name: Trastuzumab + Combined Androgen Blockade + Chemo-Hormonal Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports showing complete responses with upfront triple combination; biologically rational for dual AR+/HER2+ SDC; no prospective Phase 2 trial of triple combination

survival_benefit.rationale: Case reports of complete pathological responses with triple combination in AR+/HER2+ SDC; addresses both oncogenic drivers simultaneously; potentially superior to sequential single-pathway targeting

accessibility.rationale: All components available individually; off-label combination; requires multidisciplinary coordination between oncology subspecialties; complex regimen management

safety_profile.rationale: Triple combination adds cumulative toxicities: ADT effects + HER2-directed cardiotoxicity + chemotherapy myelosuppression; requires careful monitoring of multiple organ systems

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient is both AR+ AND HER2 3+; simultaneous targeting of both drivers is biologically optimal; dual biomarker positivity makes this patient ideal for combined approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Simultaneous targeting of both HER2 (trastuzumab) and AR (GnRH agonist + antiandrogen) pathways in dual-positive SDC, combined with cytotoxic chemotherapy. Addresses potential cross-talk between AR and HER2 signaling pathways and prevents compensatory pathway activation.

key_evidence.journal: Case Reports in Oncology / Frontiers in Oncology

treatments.name: Alpelisib + Bicalutamide (PI3K Inhibitor + Antiandrogen)

evidence_level.rationale: Case report showing excellent response in PIK3CA-mutated AR+ SDC; molecular profiling identifies actionable PIK3CA mutations in subset of SDC; preclinical rationale strong

survival_benefit.rationale: Excellent response reported in case with PIK3CA mutation; PI3K pathway frequently activated in SDC; addresses resistance mechanism to AR blockade alone

accessibility.rationale: Alpelisib FDA approved for PIK3CA-mutated breast cancer; requires molecular profiling to identify PIK3CA mutation; off-label combination; specialized centers

safety_profile.rationale: Alpelisib: hyperglycemia (dose-limiting), diarrhea, rash; bicalutamide: standard ADT effects; combination toxicity manageable but requires glucose monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires PIK3CA mutation (present in subset of SDC) plus AR+; highly targeted for molecular profile; NGS recommended to identify actionable mutations

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alpelisib selectively inhibits PI3K-alpha (mutant PIK3CA), blocking downstream AKT/mTOR signaling that drives SDC proliferation and survival. Combined with bicalutamide AR blockade to address dual oncogenic dependencies. PI3K and AR pathways cross-talk in SDC, making dual targeting rational.

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Study of Trastuzumab Deruxtecan in HER2-Positive Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Gland Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 61.3% in IHC 3+; median DOR 22.1 months; median PFS 11.9 months; ILD risk 10.5%

clinical_trials.title: Darolutamide + Goserelin vs Darolutamide in AR-Positive Salivary Gland Carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: CAB with darolutamide superior to monotherapy in response rate and PFS

clinical_trials.title: Goserelin Acetate with Pembrolizumab in AR-Positive Salivary Gland Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: Enrollment ongoing; combines immune checkpoint inhibition with androgen suppression

clinical_trials.title: T-DXd in Previously Treated HER2-Expressing Advanced Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Tumor-agnostic approval based on ORR across multiple tumor types including salivary gland cancer

clinical_trials.title: Docetaxel/Trastuzumab vs T-DM1 in HER2+ Salivary Gland Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: Head-to-head comparison of HER2-directed strategies; enrollment ongoing

clinical_trials.title: Molecular Profiling-Guided Therapy for Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Duct Carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Matches patients to targeted therapies based on actionable mutations (PIK3CA, HRAS, FGFR, NTRK, BRAF)

combination_strategies.rationale: T4aN2 disease mandates adjuvant RT; cisplatin added for positive margins; facial nerve sacrifice with immediate reanimation; comprehensive neck dissection for N2 disease; post-op RT starts 4-6 weeks after surgery

Dual AR+/HER2+ SDC benefits from simultaneous targeting of both oncogenic drivers; prevents compensatory pathway activation; case reports show complete responses; biologically optimal for patient's dual-positive profile

T-DXd provides potent HER2-directed ADC activity; adding CAB addresses AR-driven tumor cell survival; may improve depth of response in dual-positive SDC; sequential or concurrent use under investigation
Androgen suppression may alter tumor microenvironment to enhance immunotherapy response; preclinical data supports AR blockade improving PD-L1 expression; Phase 2 trial currently enrolling

HER2-positive SDC has high recurrence risk; adjuvant trastuzumab after definitive local therapy may reduce distant failure; extrapolated from HER2+ breast cancer adjuvant paradigm; under investigation

supportive_care.approach: Facial Nerve Reanimation (Immediate at Time of Sacrifice)
supportive_care.evidence: Analysis of 285 patients undergoing total parotidectomy with facial nerve sacrifice; 31.2% received concurrent reanimation; nerve grafts (sural, great auricular) and static slings available
supportive_care.benefit: Immediate reanimation at time of sacrifice produces best functional outcomes; restores eye closure (prevents corneal exposure keratopathy), oral competence, and facial symmetry; reduces duration of complete paralysis; 46% receive nerve-type repairs

supportive_care.evidence: SDC harbors multiple actionable mutations: HER2 amplification (40-83%), AR expression (73-89%), PIK3CA mutations (20-30%), HRAS mutations (5-15%), NTRK fusions (rare); NCCN recommends molecular testing
supportive_care.benefit: Identifies all targetable alterations beyond HER2 and AR; guides sequential therapy options; identifies PIK3CA mutations for alpelisib; screens for NTRK fusions (larotrectinib/entrectinib eligible); informs clinical trial eligibility

supportive_care.evidence: Trastuzumab cardiotoxicity: LVEF decline in 5-15% of patients; dose-dependent and usually reversible; baseline and interval LVEF assessment every 3 months
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of HER2-targeted therapy cardiotoxicity; enables dose modification or treatment interruption before symptomatic heart failure; particularly important with concurrent anthracycline use

supportive_care.approach: Eye Care and Corneal Protection (Post Facial Nerve Sacrifice)
supportive_care.evidence: Facial nerve sacrifice causes loss of blink reflex and incomplete eye closure; corneal exposure keratopathy develops rapidly without intervention
supportive_care.benefit: Artificial tears, moisture chambers, and tarsorrhaphy prevent sight-threatening corneal desiccation and ulceration; gold weight implant in upper eyelid provides passive eye closure; ophthalmology consultation mandatory post-operatively

supportive_care.approach: Bone Density Monitoring and Bisphosphonate Therapy (For ADT)
supportive_care.evidence: Androgen deprivation therapy accelerates bone loss; DEXA scan recommended at baseline and annually; bisphosphonates or denosumab prevent osteoporotic fractures
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents ADT-induced osteoporosis and pathologic fractures; denosumab 60 mg every 6 months or zoledronic acid 5 mg annually; calcium and vitamin D supplementation recommended
